title: Engineered protein delivery platform and use thereof for antibacterial treatments
abstract: Provided herein is a non-pathogenic bacterium comprising a gene cassette encoding an antibacterial protein delivery platform, wherein the gene cassette is operably linked to a positive regulator inducibly-expressed from a genomic location.
claims:
Claims 1. A genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium having antibacterial activity, comprising a gene cluster encoding an antibacterial protein delivery platform for producing antibacterial activity, operably linked to a signal-inducible positive regulation system, and at least one effector-immunity pair, delivery of which exerts the antibacterial activity wherein the gene cluster and the positive regulation system are localized in different genomic loci within the non-pathogenic bacterium. 2. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the positive regulation system comprises at least one activator associated with at least one promoter, configured to activate the antibacterial protein delivery platform to produce the antibacterial activity, wherein the at least one activator is a signal receptor/regulator configured to associate with at least one signal activated promoter, upon sensing an activating signal. 3. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 2, wherein the positive regulation system further comprises a plurality of regulators, each associated with at least one promotor. 4. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 3, wherein the positive regulation system further comprises at least one gene cluster activator, and wherein the at least one signal activated promoter induces expression of the at least one gene cluster activator, which upon association with at least one gene cluster promoter within the gene cluster, activates the antibacterial protein delivery platform to produce antibacterial activity. 5. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 2, wherein the gene cluster, the signal receptor/regulator and the at least one effector-immunity pair are localized in different genomic loci within the non-pathogenic bacterium. 6. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 4, wherein the gene cluster, the signal receptor/regulator, the at least one gene cluster activator and the at least one effector-immunity pair are localized in different genomic loci within the non-pathogenic bacterium. 33 

The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the activating signal is an external signal exerted by a pathogen. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the at least one effector-immunity pair and the gene cluster are derived from the same bacterial strain. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the at least one effector-immunity pair and the gene cluster are derived from different bacterial strains. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the gene cluster is encoding an antibacterial Type VI secretion system (T6SS) devoid of a T6SS positive regulation system and T6SS effectors-immunity pairs. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein the gene cluster is derived from a pathogen. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1 , wherein said the bacterial strain from which the genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium is derived, is devoid of an endogenous T6SS. The genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1, wherein the bacterial strain from which the genetically engineered non-pathogenic bacterium is derived, is a marine bacterium. The non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1, wherein the gene cluster is located on a plasmid. The non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 14, wherein the at least one effector-immunity pair is located on a chromosome. The non-pathogenic bacterium according to claim 1, wherein the gene cluster and the at least one effector-immunity pair are located on the same or different chromosomes in the non-pathogenic bacterium. A composition comprising the non-pathogenic bacterium according to any one of claims 1 to 16 and a carrier. 34 

 The composition according to claim 17, being in a dry, lyophilized, form. The composition according to claim 17, being in a bacteria culture form. A method of treating pathogen-infected organisms, the method comprising a. contacting the infected organisms with a composition comprising the non- pathogenic bacteria of claim 1 , wherein the at least one effector-immunity pair is capable of reducing or eliminating activity or proliferation of the pathogens; and b. activating the positive regulation system, thereby inducing the non-pathogenic bacteria to exert antibacterial activity. The method according to claim 20, wherein the infected organisms are marine organisms in an aqueous environment. The method according to claim 21, wherein said contacting comprises adding the composition to the aqueous environment. The method according to claim 20, wherein the gene cluster is encoding an antibacterial Type VI secretion system (T6SS). The method according to claim 23, wherein the T6SS is derived from marine pathogen. The method according to claim 20, wherein said non-pathogenic bacterium is devoid of an endogenous T6SS. The method according to claim 20, wherein said non-pathogenic bacterium is a marine bacterium. The method according to claim 20, wherein the gene cluster encoding the antibacterial protein delivery platform is derived from a pathogenic bacterium. The method according to claim 20, wherein the gene cluster and the at least one effector-immunity pair are located on the same or different chromosomes in the non- pathogenic bacterium. 29. The method according to claim 20, wherein the external signal is a bacterial pathogen, or a molecule derived therefrom, present within the aqueous environment. 30. The method according to claim 20, wherein the external signal is a compound added to the aqueous environment, in order to activate the antibacterial protein delivery platform. 31. A kit comprising at least one receptacle containing the composition of any one of claims 17 to 19, for treating infected organisms and environments, and further comprising instructions for use. 32. The kit according to claim 31, further comprising a positive control configured to verify that the composition in the at least one receptacle, is active. 33. The kit according to claim 32, wherein the positive control comprises a sample of an activating signal molecule and a detector of secreted platform components.
